name: Ann Kathleen Mckay's Strawberry Cake a La Dacquoise
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Cup Eau de Vie Framboise (Raspberry Brandy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Genoise according to the recipe elsewhere in this database.
2. Cut one of the 9-inch genoises into two layers for the top, and use the other whole for the bottom.
3. Lightly sprinkle each genoise layer with 2 tablespoons of Raspberry Brandy.
4. Flavor 2 cups of buttercream frosting with 1 tablespoon of Raspberry Brandy.
5. Whip Creme Fraiche and Heavy Cream together until stiff peaks form.
6. Add 1/4 cup of Creme Anglaise and 1 tablespoon of Raspberry Brandy, then whip again until stiff peaks form.
7. If strawberries are small, slice them lengthwise; if larger, cut crosswise into 1/2-inch slices.
8. Spread the bottom genoise layer with one-third of the buttercream. Arrange sliced berries around the perimeter, cut side out, and continue covering with half the berries and whipped cream-creme anglaise mixture.
9. Repeat for the second genoise layer, then place the final layer on top and spread a thin layer of buttercream, smoothing nicely.
10. Pipe a decorative border around the top with remaining buttercream (add food coloring if desired).
11. Arrange Wild Strawberries along the perimeter, garnish with leaves and flowers.
12. Refrigerate for 2-4 hours before serving.
